title: Sweet And Spicy Uruguayan Sugar Stir-fry
description: A dense and sour one-pot dish from Uruguay featuring ground sugar as the star ingredient, cooked with spices to achieve a unique flavor profile that's both sweet and spicy.
instructions: Preheat your broiler according to manufacturer’s instructions, ensuring it reaches a temperature of at least 450°F.
In a large skillet or non-stick pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add the ground sugar and stir until it melts completely and turns amber brown.
Add red pepper flakes, garlic powder, salt, turmeric powder, and paprika to the melted sugar. Stir well so that all ingredients are evenly distributed and flavored.
Carefully add the baby carrots into the skillet with the sugar mixture. Spread them out in an even layer to ensure even cooking.
Broil for about 8-10 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender-crisp and lightly caramelized. Use tongs to gently toss the vegetables as they cook to ensure even distribution of heat.
Remove from the oven once cooked, season with additional salt if needed, and serve hot.
